meta.title: Poetry Collection — Vocabulary Set 3
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Part 2 words focus: cardboard, cave, dragon, carved, squishy, scoop, moat, decorate (from 'The Box' and 'Sandcastle').

questions.question: What is 'cardboard'?
options: A thick, stiff type of paper used for boxes
A type of wood from trees
questions.explanation: 'Cardboard' is a thick, stiff material made from paper. Boxes, cereal packets, and moving containers are often made from cardboard.

questions.question: A 'cave' is a large open space inside a rock or hill.
questions.explanation: Yes! A 'cave' is a hollow space — like a tunnel or room — inside rock or a hillside. In 'The Box,' the child imagines the cardboard box as a 'cardboard cave.'

questions.question: The wet sand felt _______ and soft when I squeezed it in my hand.
questions.hint: Soft and wet when you squeeze it
questions.explanation: 'Squishy' means soft and yielding when squeezed — like wet sand, mud, or a sponge. 'Sandcastle' describes the sand as 'soft and squishy, wet and brown.'

questions.question: Dad 'carved' a hole in the box. What does 'carved' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Carved' is the past tense of 'carve' — to cut carefully with a tool. Dad carved a hole — cut it out neatly — to make a window in the box.

questions.question: A _______ is a deep ditch of water built around a castle for protection.
questions.hint: A water ditch surrounding a castle
questions.explanation: A 'moat' is a defensive water ditch built around a castle. 'In Sandcastle,' the children build a moat around their sand castle — copying a real castle design!

questions.question: We use a shovel to _______ sand into the bucket.
questions.explanation: 'Scoop' means to pick up or gather something with a curved tool or hands. 'Scoop sand into the bucket' means using a scooping motion to fill it up.

questions.question: 'Decorate' means to take away things to make something simpler.
questions.explanation: 'Decorate' means to ADD things to make something look more beautiful. You decorate a castle with shells and pretty stones — you add, not take away.

questions.question: In stories, a _______ is a large, scary creature that can breathe fire.
questions.explanation: A 'dragon' is a mythical (imaginary) creature from stories and legends, often shown as large, scary, and able to breathe fire. In 'The Box,' the child imagines fighting one.

questions.question: Sort these Part 2 words — VERBS (actions) or NOUNS (things)?
questions.explanation: 'Carved' (cut), 'scoop' (pick up), and 'decorate' (add beauty to) are actions. 'Moat,' 'dragon,' and 'cave' are things or places.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a line from 'Sandcastle':
questions.explanation: Correct line: 'A moat surrounds it all.' The moat goes all the way around the sandcastle — 'surrounds' means goes around all sides.

questions.question: Why is the box called a 'cardboard cave'?
options: Because it is made of real cave stone
Because it is hollow and dark inside, like a cave
Because it is very large and heavy
questions.explanation: A cave is a hollow, dark space inside rock. The cardboard box is also hollow and dark inside — so the child imagines it as a 'cardboard cave.' This is a creative comparison (metaphor).

questions.question: When the poem calls the box a 'cardboard cave,' it is using a METAPHOR — saying one thing IS another thing to spark imagination.
questions.explanation: Yes! A metaphor says one thing IS another. 'Cardboard cave' = the box IS a cave (in the child's imagination). It is not literally a cave — it is a cardboard box, but the metaphor makes it feel like an adventure.

questions.question: She carefully _______ the birthday cake with candles, flowers, and ribbons.
questions.hint: Past tense of decorate — added beautiful things
questions.explanation: 'Decorated' (past tense) means added beautiful or festive things to make something look better. 'She decorated the cake' means she added colorful items to make it look special.

questions.question: Which word could REPLACE 'squishy' in the sentence 'The sand felt squishy'?
questions.explanation: 'Squishy' = soft and mushy when pressed. 'The sand felt soft and mushy' means the same thing — wet sand that gives way when you press it.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about 'The Box':
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The child crawled inside the cardboard cave.' The child crawled (moved on hands and knees) inside the box that she imagined as a cave.
